title: Magical Girl and Colored Liquid Potions

content.problemStatement:
Naturally, the magical girl is very good at performing magic. She recently met her master wizard Devu, who gifted her **R** potions of red liquid, 
**B** potions of blue liquid, and **G** potions of green liquid.






The red liquid potions have liquid amounts given by **r[1], ..., r[R]** liters. 


The green liquid potions have liquid amounts given by **g[1], ..., g[G]** liters. 


The blue liquid potions have liquid amounts given by **b[1], ..., b[B]** liters. 


She want to play with the potions by applying magic tricks on them. In a single magic trick, she will choose a particular color. Then she will pick all the potions of the chosen color and decrease the amount of liquid in them to half (i.e. if initial amount 
of liquid is **x**, then the amount after decrement will be **x / 2 ** where division is integer division, e.g. 3 / 2 = 1 and 4 / 2 = 2). 


Because she has to go out of station to meet her uncle Churu, a wannabe wizard, only **M** minutes are left for her. In a single minute, she can perform at most one magic trick. Hence, she can perform at most **M** magic tricks.



She would like to minimize the maximum amount of liquid among all of Red, Green and Blue colored potions. Formally Let v be the maximum value of amount of liquid in any potion. We want to minimize the value of v.
Please help her.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains four integers R, G, B, M on the first line, followed by R integers for red potions, G integers for green potions, and B integers for blue potions.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimum maximum amount of liquid among all of Red, Green, and Blue colored potions after applying the magic tricks.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1. Magical girl can pick the blue potion and make its liquid amount half. So the potions will now have amounts 1 2 1. Maximum of these values is 2. Hence answer is 2.

Explanation:
Example case 2. Magical girl can pick the red potion and make its liquid amount half. So the potions will now have amounts 2 8 2 4 6 8. Maximum of these values is 8. Then she can pick the blue potion and make its liquid amount half. So the potions will now have amounts 2 8 2 4 3 4. Maximum of these values is 8. Then she can pick the green potion and make its liquid amount half. So the potions will now have amounts 2 8 1 2 3 4. Maximum of these values is 8. Then she can pick the blue potion and make its liquid amount half. So the potions will now have amounts 2 8 1 2 1 2. Maximum of these values is 8. Hence answer is 4.

Explanation:
Example case 3. Magical girl can pick the red potion and make its liquid amount half. So the potions will now have amounts 5 20 30. Maximum of these values is 30. Then she can pick the blue potion and make its liquid amount half. So the potions will now have amounts 5 20 15. Maximum of these values is 20. Hence answer is 10.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using binary search. We need to find the minimum maximum amount of liquid among all potions after applying the magic tricks. We can start by finding the maximum amount of liquid among all potions and then perform a binary search to find the minimum maximum amount.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * (R + G + B) * log(max_val)) per test case, where max_val is the maximum amount of liquid among all potions.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(R + G + B) for storing the potions.
